name: FriendlyFace ForensicSeal
description: Verifiable AI Compliance Certificates — the SSL certificate for AI systems. Cryptographically signed, publicly verifiable compliance credentials backed by real forensic evidence, targeting EU AI Act enforcement August 2, 2026.

userStories.title: ForensicSeal Issuance Endpoint
userStories.description: As a compliance officer, I want to request a ForensicSeal for my AI system so that I have a cryptographically verifiable proof of compliance.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /seal/issue accepts {system_id, system_name, assessment_scope, bundle_ids[]}
Endpoint runs compliance checks across all 6 layers: consent coverage, bias audit recency, explanation coverage, hash chain integrity, bundle verification, provenance completeness
Each check produces a pass/fail with score (0-100) and evidence references
If all checks pass threshold (configurable, default 80%), issues a ForensicSeal
ForensicSeal is a W3C Verifiable Credential signed with the platform's Ed25519 DID:key
Credential contains: issuer DID, subject system ID, issuance timestamp, expiry (default 90 days), compliance score, ZK proof of underlying evidence, Merkle root at time of issuance
Seal is persisted to DB with unique seal_id via new seals table migration
Returns JSON with seal_id, credential, verification_url, expires_at, compliance_summary
If checks fail, returns 422 with detailed failure report and remediation steps
pytest tests/ -v passes
ruff check friendlyface/ tests/ passes
userStories.notes: Core product endpoint. Reuses existing crypto/did.py, crypto/vc.py, crypto/schnorr.py, governance/compliance.py. Add new seals table to DB via migration. Add new ForensicSeal OpenAPI tag.

userStories.title: ForensicSeal Public Verification
userStories.description: As a regulator or auditor, I want to verify a ForensicSeal without needing an account so that I can independently confirm an AI system's compliance status.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /seal/verify accepts {credential} (the full VC JSON)
GET /seal/verify/{seal_id} verifies by seal ID (public, no auth)
Verification checks: DID signature valid, credential not expired, credential not revoked, ZK proof valid, Merkle root matches chain state at issuance time
Returns {valid: bool, checks: {signature, expiry, revocation, zk_proof, merkle}, issuer, issued_at, expires_at, compliance_score}
No API key required — these endpoints are fully public
Verification works offline with just the credential JSON (DID + ZK proof are self-contained)
pytest tests/ -v passes
ruff check friendlyface/ tests/ passes
userStories.notes: Public-facing verification. Must work without auth. Critical for trust model — anyone can verify a seal.

userStories.title: Seal Expiry and Continuous Compliance
userStories.description: As a compliance officer, I want seals to expire and require re-issuance so that compliance is continuous, not point-in-time.
acceptanceCriteria: Seals have configurable expiry (default 90 days, min 7, max 365)
GET /seal/status/{seal_id} returns current status including days until expiry
Expired seals automatically marked as expired on access
POST /seal/renew/{seal_id} re-runs compliance checks and issues a new seal if passing
Renewal links to previous seal via previous_seal_id, creating a compliance chain
SSE event emitted when seal is within 30 days of expiry: seal_expiring
pytest tests/ -v passes
ruff check friendlyface/ tests/ passes
userStories.notes: Continuous compliance is the business model — recurring value from re-issuance.

userStories.title: Seal Revocation
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to revoke a seal if compliance is breached so that the public verification endpoint reflects current reality.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /seal/revoke/{seal_id} with {reason} marks seal as revoked
Revocation is itself a forensic event in the hash chain (event_type: seal_revoked)
Public verification endpoint returns valid: false with revocation_reason for revoked seals
Revocation is irreversible — no un-revoke
Admin role required via require_role('admin') dependency
pytest tests/ -v passes
ruff check friendlyface/ tests/ passes
userStories.notes: Completes the seal lifecycle: issue -> verify -> renew -> revoke.

userStories.title: Compliance Proxy Middleware
userStories.description: As an enterprise running NEC/Cognitec/Amazon Rekognition, I want to route my API calls through FriendlyFace so that every recognition request is automatically forensically logged without changing my integration.
acceptanceCriteria: New endpoint POST /proxy/recognize accepts {upstream_url, upstream_headers, image, metadata}
Proxy forwards the image to the upstream recognition API using httpx.AsyncClient
Before forwarding: checks consent for subject (if subject_id in metadata), logs inference_request forensic event
After response: logs inference_result forensic event with upstream response, input image hash, latency_ms
Accumulated results feed into bias audits and compliance scoring
Configuration via FF_PROXY_UPSTREAM_URL and FF_PROXY_UPSTREAM_KEY env vars for default upstream
Works with any REST-based recognition API (vendor-agnostic)
pytest tests/ -v passes
ruff check friendlyface/ tests/ passes
userStories.notes: This is 'you keep your recognition engine, we make it compliant'. Uses httpx.AsyncClient (already a dependency).

userStories.title: EU AI Act Annex IV Conformity Document Generator
userStories.description: As a compliance officer, I want to generate a full Annex IV technical documentation package so that I can demonstrate conformity before the August 2, 2026 deadline.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /governance/conformity-assessment generates a structured document

Document covers all Annex IV requirements: system description, intended purpose, risk classification, training data provenance, bias testing records, performance metrics, human oversight measures, cybersecurity measures, quality management procedures

Each section auto-populated from FriendlyFace data: provenance DAG for training data, bias audits for fairness, forensic bundles for integrity, consent records for data protection, compliance reports for risk management
Output formats: JSON (structured) and HTML (human-readable) via Accept header or format query param
Gaps identified and flagged: missing sections listed with action items
Document references specific ForensicSeal IDs as evidence when available
pytest tests/ -v passes
ruff check friendlyface/ tests/ passes
userStories.notes: The document enterprises need to file before August 2, 2026. Extends existing governance/compliance.py.

userStories.title: Pluggable Recognition Engine Interface
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to swap the recognition engine without touching the forensic layer so that we can support production-grade models alongside the demo PCA+SVM.
acceptanceCriteria: Define RecognitionEngine ABC in friendlyface/recognition/engine.py with train(), predict(), get_model_info() methods
FallbackEngine wraps existing PCA+SVM pipeline — current behavior fully preserved
ONNXEngine loads ArcFace/AdaFace ONNX models for production inference
ProxyEngine delegates to the compliance proxy (US-005) for external APIs
Engine selection via FF_RECOGNITION_ENGINE=fallback|onnx|proxy env var
All engines produce identical forensic events — forensic layer is engine-agnostic
Existing tests pass with fallback engine (default)
pytest tests/ -v passes
ruff check friendlyface/ tests/ passes
userStories.notes: Decouples recognition from forensics. The forensic layer wraps any engine.

userStories.title: Blockchain Merkle Root Anchoring
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want to periodically anchor Merkle roots to a public blockchain so that the forensic chain has immutable, externally verifiable timestamps.
acceptanceCriteria: New module friendlyface/crypto/anchor.py with BlockchainAnchor ABC
PolygonAnchor implementation: publishes Merkle root hash to a Polygon smart contract
BaseAnchor implementation: publishes to Base L2
NullAnchor (default): no-op for local/demo deployments
Anchoring triggered on configurable interval (default: every 100 events or 24 hours)
Anchor transaction hash stored in DB via new anchors table
GET /anchor/history returns list of anchored roots with tx hashes and block numbers
ForensicSeal includes latest anchor tx hash as external proof reference
Configuration via FF_ANCHOR_CHAIN=none|polygon|base and FF_ANCHOR_KEY env vars
pytest tests/ -v passes
ruff check friendlyface/ tests/ passes
userStories.notes: Optional module — system works fully without it. Keep blockchain deps (web3) in optional dependency group in pyproject.toml.

userStories.title: Python SDK for Enterprise Integration
userStories.description: As an enterprise developer, I want a pip-installable SDK so that I can add forensic logging to my existing pipeline in 5 lines of code.
acceptanceCriteria: New package in sdk/ directory with its own pyproject.toml (name: friendlyface-sdk)
Core class: FriendlyFaceClient(base_url, api_key)
Methods: log_event(), check_consent(), grant_consent(), create_bundle(), issue_seal(), verify_seal(), run_audit(), proxy_recognize()
Decorator: @forensic_trace wraps any function, logs inputs/outputs as forensic events
Context manager: with client.forensic_session() as session: auto-bundles all events on exit
Returns typed dataclasses, not raw dicts
README.md with quickstart showing 5-line integration example
90%+ test coverage on SDK (pytest sdk/tests/ -v)
Zero required dependencies beyond requests
ruff check sdk/ passes
userStories.notes: Thin HTTP wrapper — the SDK is the distribution mechanism for enterprise adoption. Separate package, separate tests.

userStories.title: ForensicSeal Dashboard Page
userStories.description: As a platform user, I want a dashboard showing all issued seals, their status, and expiry so that I can manage my compliance posture.
acceptanceCriteria: New /seals page in React frontend added to sidebar navigation
Shows table of all issued seals: seal_id (truncated+copy), system_name, issued_at, expires_at, status, compliance_score
Status badges: green (active >30 days), yellow (expiring <30 days), red (expired/revoked)
Click seal row to see full compliance breakdown with per-layer scores and evidence links
Issue New Seal button opens inline form triggering POST /seal/issue
Verify External Seal input field for pasting credential JSON with verify button
Seal history chart showing compliance score over time (inline CSS bars, no chart library)
Skeleton loading states and empty state when no seals exist
cd frontend && npm run build && npm run lint passes
pytest tests/ -v passes
ruff check friendlyface/ tests/ passes
userStories.notes: UI story. Must be responsive, dark theme consistent with existing dashboard pages.

userStories.title: ForensicSeal Landing Page
userStories.description: As a potential customer, I want a compelling landing page explaining ForensicSeal so that I understand the value and can request a demo.
acceptanceCriteria: New route /seal in React frontend serves a public landing page
Hero section: 'The compliance certificate your AI system needs before August 2, 2026'
Sections: What is ForensicSeal, How it works (3-step visual), Why you need it (EU AI Act penalties), Competitive comparison vs manual audits, Pricing placeholder, Demo request form
Live verification demo: paste any seal credential JSON, see verification result in real-time via POST /seal/verify
Responsive layout, dark theme consistent with existing dashboard
SEO meta tags for 'EU AI Act compliance', 'AI compliance certificate', 'forensic AI'
cd frontend && npm run build && npm run lint passes
pytest tests/ -v passes
ruff check friendlyface/ tests/ passes
userStories.notes: UI story. Customer-facing landing page. Must be compelling enough to drive demo requests.

userStories.title: ICDF2C 2026 Paper Submission Package
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want to finalize the academic paper with real benchmarks from the activated pipeline so that we can submit to ICDF2C 2026.
acceptanceCriteria: Run scripts/full_pipeline_demo.py with timing instrumentation added, collect latency metrics for each step
Benchmarks collected: event recording latency, bundle creation time, ZK proof generation time, seal issuance time, verification time, Merkle proof time
Update docs/PAPER_DRAFT.md Table 3 (Performance) with real measured numbers
Add ForensicSeal as a new contribution in Section 4 (Implementation)
Generate comparison table vs 5 commercial systems with ForensicSeal column added
Export paper as LaTeX (.tex) file in docs/ for conference submission
Prepare supplementary materials: architecture diagrams, API documentation link, demo URL
pytest tests/ -v passes
ruff check friendlyface/ tests/ passes
userStories.notes: Depends on ForensicSeal being implemented so benchmarks include real seal issuance/verification times.
